metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` as an edit target (or explicitly document an alternative eval execution path) to unblock running the code-review-tests eval required by AC2.
findings.finding: `eval_solver.run_case()` (the only eval harness in the codebase) has no dispatch arm for `code-review-tests` or any code-review overlay prompt id. It handles `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`, and plan-review criteria only; all other prompt ids fall through to `raise ValueError(f'no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}')`. Running the recall + no-fire eval required by AC2 would fail at runtime without adding a new dispatch arm — an unenumerated consumer the plan does not mention.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: AC2 — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the eval harness will invoke the `code-review-tests` overlay (e.g. extend `eval_solver.run_case` with a code-review-overlay arm that feeds a diff fixture to the overlay op), or explicitly note that a new solver arm is a deliverable of this story.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 ('Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') requires eval_solver.run_case('code-review-tests', ...), but the eval_solver currently supports only three agentic reviewer IDs (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment) and plan-review criteria — 'code-review-tests' falls into neither arm and would raise ValueError('no eval solver for prompt ...'). No eval spec file exists yet and the plan does not describe how this solver gap will be closed.
findings.location: AC: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:106-171
findings.suggested_fix: Add a deliverable: extend `eval_solver.run_case` with a `code-review-tests` (or generic code-review-overlay) arm that stands up a disposable diff fixture and invokes the overlay op, parallel to the existing agentic-reviewer arms. Alternatively, explicitly scope the eval to an offline structural validation (spec-validates + scorer-passes on a FakeRunner) and document what the live eval CI run requires.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a concrete eval threshold for AC2: specify the minimum number of recall fixtures per pattern (e.g., ≥1 per named pattern = 5 total), the maximum FP rate on no-fire cases (e.g., 0 FPs), and the command to run the eval (e.g., `pytest evals/tests_overlay/`) so 'passing' is falsifiable.
findings.finding: The eval spec and fixtures are listed as a deliverable but no threshold defines what a 'passing' eval means — number of recall fixtures required, minimum recall rate, maximum FP rate, or the command/runner used to execute the eval are all unspecified, blocking an executor from knowing when the work is done.
findings.location: AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.' and Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the tests overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Passing = all five recall-pattern fixtures elicit a finding, all no-fire fixtures elicit no finding, verified by running `<eval-runner-command>`. Over-suppression = any VALID-pattern fixture suppressed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the full vocabulary for each named FP-guard protocol in the plan or a referenced source document: what the 'Four-Criterion test' criteria are, what 'Rule-5 structural-artifact exception' covers, and the explicit list of 'VALID-patterns-not-to-flag' — so an implementer can embed them verbatim in `code-review-tests.md`.
findings.finding: The plan names three FP-guard protocols ('Four-Criterion test', 'Rule-5 structural-artifact exception', 'VALID-patterns-not-to-flag') that must be embedded in the overlay, but their value vocabulary — what each criterion is, what Rule-5 covers, and which patterns are valid-not-to-flag — is defined nowhere in the plan text or in any reachable codebase file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either define (or cite a canonical location for) the 'Base NOT-flag rules' that the tests overlay is exempted from, and co-locate that reference in `code-review-tests.md` alongside the carve-out rule.
findings.finding: The plan states 'Base NOT-flag rules do not apply to test-correctness findings' as a decision rule, but 'Base NOT-flag rules' are never defined in the plan or anywhere in the codebase, making the rule uninterpretable and non-co-located with the overlay that must apply it.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — last sentence: 'Base NOT-flag rules do not apply to test-correctness findings.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing the 'overlay-authoring + eval pattern' contract (overlay holds recall patterns + FP-guards; verifier stays domain-agnostic; eval spec required per overlay) so the five follow-on overlays have a stable reference.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel 'overlay-authoring + eval pattern' intended to be replicated across five additional overlays, but no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc is planned to capture this architectural decision for future maintainers.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/adr/overlay-authoring-pattern.md` (or a section in CLAUDE.md) that records: (1) the overlay/verifier split rationale, (2) the required overlay structure (recall patterns + FP-guards), and (3) the eval-spec requirement. Reference it in the plan's Files list.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC2) that explicitly verifies the overlay does not suppress valid test-correctness findings under base NOT-flag rules — e.g. include a no-fire fixture where a base NOT-flag rule would normally suppress but the test-correctness finding must still fire.
findings.finding: The behavioral rule 'Base NOT-flag rules do not apply to test-correctness findings' is stated in the What section but has no corresponding acceptance criterion to verify it was implemented correctly in the overlay.
findings.location: What section ('Base NOT-flag rules do not apply to test-correctness findings') vs. Acceptance Criteria (all three lines)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and cite the concrete, locatable artifact (file path, document, or section) that constitutes 'dso content' for test-correctness recall patterns and FP-guards, so the enrichment can be verified against it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Enrich the tests overlay prompt with dso content' treating 'dso content' as a locatable, authoritative source for test-correctness recall patterns and FP-guards, but no such artifact exists in the repo: 'dso' appears only as a corpus: tag in eval YAML files (a customer dataset label), not as a document or rubric defining the named patterns.
findings.location: What section — 'Enrich the tests overlay prompt with dso content'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'dso content' with a direct file citation (e.g. 'docs/experiments/code-review-gate/test-patterns.md' or equivalent) or inline the full enumerated list of recall patterns + FP-guards directly in the plan so the deliverable is self-contained.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval spec format for `code-review-tests.eval.yaml`: which scorers (at minimum `recall_on_seeded_defects` + `no_fire_on_good_cases`), the gate expression (e.g. `at_least(k)`), required gold_set size, and the fallback/error behavior if the spec fails validation.
findings.finding: The plan's second deliverable ('eval spec + fixtures for the tests overlay (recall + no-fire cases)') is a named sub-step with no specified format, no fallback, and no file that currently exists — the eval spec file `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` is entirely absent from `eval_specs/`.
findings.location: Files section — 'eval spec + fixtures for the tests overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'; AC2 — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Unify terminology: replace 'FP-discrimination guards' in AC1 with 'FP-guards' (or vice-versa) to match the What section's usage throughout.
findings.finding: Terminology drifts between the What section ('FP-guards') and AC1 ('FP-discrimination guards') — two different names for the same concept in the same plan.
findings.location: What section vs. Acceptance Criteria line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC1 to: 'tests overlay carries both recall patterns and FP-guards (both halves).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ground the 'locked principle' assertion by citing its location (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or a named section in an existing design doc); if it doesn't yet exist as a written rule, add creating that record to the plan's deliverables.
findings.finding: The plan invokes a 'locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier' as a settled architectural constraint, but no document, ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, or code comment in the repo establishes or names this as a 'locked' principle.
findings.location: What section — 'per the locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that no existing code-review-tests.eval.yaml exists (it doesn't), then explicitly scope the eval spec creation as a net-new deliverable with its required fields (prompt, epochs, gate, scorers, dataset with recall + no-fire cases) spelled out in the plan.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' but no code-review-tests.eval.yaml exists in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (or anywhere in the repo), and the plan's Files section lists it only as 'eval spec + fixtures for the tests overlay' without confirming any existing skeleton or harness to build from.
findings.location: AC2 — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'; Files section — 'eval spec + fixtures for the tests overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection under Files describing the eval spec's required structure (mirroring plan-review-finder.eval.yaml: prompt id, epochs:3, at_least gate, recall dataset cases with expect:finding, no-fire cases with expect:pass) so the deliverable is fully specified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete file path for the eval spec artifact (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml`) in the Files section so the creation target is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The second file artifact in the 'Files' section — 'eval spec + fixtures for the tests overlay (recall + no-fire cases)' — has no concrete path specified, and no such file exists anywhere in the repo (no `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` in `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` or elsewhere). The plan names it as a deliverable but an implementer has no canonical location to create it.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the tests overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml` to the Files list, following the naming convention of the existing `completion-verifier.eval.yaml`, `ticket-quality.eval.yaml`, etc.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
